architecture.summary: CoT-RAG combines chain-of-thought reasoning with retrieval-augmented generation by: (1) using knowledge graphs to modulate and guide LLM reasoning chain generation, (2) retrieving relevant sub-cases and sub-descriptions from knowledge graphs via learnable RAG to provide grounding, and (3) prompting LLMs to execute reasoning as pseudo-programs for logical rigor. Information flows from knowledge graphs → CoT generation → RAG retrieval → pseudo-program prompting → LLM execution.
trained_components: learnable RAG retriever for knowledge case selection
knowledge graph encoding for CoT modulation

biblio.title: CoT-RAG: Integrating Chain of Thought and Retrieval-Augmented Generation to Enhance Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: Evaluations on nine public datasets spanning three reasoning tasks reveal significant accuracy gains ranging from 4.0% to 44.3% over state-of-the-art methods
Knowledge Graph-driven CoT Generation features knowledge graphs to modulate reasoning chain generation of LLMs, thereby enhancing reasoning credibility
Learnable Knowledge Case-aware RAG incorporates retrieval-augmented generation into knowledge graphs to retrieve relevant sub-cases and sub-descriptions, providing LLMs with learnable information
Pseudo-Program Prompting Execution promotes greater logical rigor by guiding LLMs to execute reasoning tasks as pseudo-programs
Tests on four domain-specific datasets demonstrate exceptional accuracy and efficient execution, underscoring practical applicability and scalability

Chain-of-thought reasoning faces limitations including lack of reliability when solely relying on LLM-generated reasoning chains and lower reasoning performance from natural language prompts compared with code prompts
